column1: # we emit exactly the SYMBOL portion (everything between the $ and the first
# -locale suffix) as a literal at its position, and drop the locale id. A pure
# locale tag [$-409] has an empty symbol (contributes no literal). A bare
# [$USD] (no -locale) emits the whole "USD". The locale id is NO LONGER dropped:
# as of the M3 LOCALIZATION track (spec §9, D-8; registry locale.yaml row
# RENDERED separators for the D-8 v1 set (en/de). LCID 407 (de-DE) and 807
# (de-CH) localize to "," decimal + "." group; every other LCID (409 en-US, 809
# en-GB, 411 ja, 414 nb, …) stays en grouping (en is the unmodelled-LCID
# fallback). So [$€-407]#,##0 of 1234 is now €1.234 (de), matching Excel de.
